photos.caption: Les Fleurs
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this beautiful arrangement of lavender and violet flowers, possibly Agapanthus, standing tall against a bright, textured ground surface. The delicate petals and budding flowers are in sharp focus, showcasing their gentle colors and forms. The light beige background with its subtle linear pattern provides a lovely, clean contrast, making the vibrant blues and purples of the blossoms really pop. It feels like a serene moment, perhaps encountered during a walk or in a garden, where I paused to appreciate the simple elegance of nature's design.

photos.caption: Splash
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this dynamic shot of splashing water, perhaps from a fountain, a wave, or a fast-flowing stream. The monochromatic tones really highlight the intricate textures and the powerful, chaotic movement of the water as it crashes and disperses. I was drawn to the abstract beauty of the spray and bubbles, creating this vivid pattern against the dark background. It's a moment frozen in time, emphasizing the raw energy and ephemeral nature of water.

photos.caption: Starfish
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this striking illuminated star. It's a beautiful, multi-pointed paper lantern, glowing warmly with small lights that create wonderful dotted patterns and highlight the star cutouts. The black and white filter really emphasizes the contrast between the radiant light and the dark background, making it feel quite atmospheric. It was likely a decorative piece, possibly in my home or somewhere I visited, and I must have been drawn to its intricate design and the magical glow it cast.

photos.caption: Aud's fourth birthday: 2
photos.captionKii: I remember this cozy moment with my family on the couch. Everyone is gathered around, intently focused on whatever the woman on the left is holding in her hands, which looks like a small object or device. The little girl with blonde hair in her striped pajamas, with 'COOKIES MAKE' on her shirt, is looking up at it with such curiosity, while the older girl in red seems equally engrossed. The boy in the background is also observant. It's these relaxed, intimate moments at home, just sharing a simple discovery or activity, that I love to capture – a true picture of togetherness.

photos.caption: Aud's fourth birthday: 1
photos.captionKii: I remember this cozy moment with my family on the couch. It looks like we were all gathered around, maybe looking at something interesting on a phone or small device that the woman on the left is holding. Everyone's expressions are great; the two girls are smiling broadly, especially the youngest one with her adorable curly blonde hair and striped leggings, looking off to the side with such joy. The boy in the plaid pants looks quite engrossed too. It's always nice to capture these relaxed, intimate moments at home, just enjoying each other's company and sharing a laugh or discovery.

photos.caption: Aud's fourth birthday: 3
photos.captionKii: I remember this heartwarming scene with my family, gathered on the couch in our living room. It looks like a special moment, possibly a birthday celebration, as the children are focused on a book or card held by the boy, which clearly shows the number '4'. The youngest one, in her cute 'COOKIES MAKE HAPPY' top, is looking down at a purple wrapped gift in her lap with such anticipation. My daughter in the red shirt is also leaning in, engrossed in the moment. It's these intimate and joyful family gatherings, full of shared curiosity and delight, that I truly cherish and love to capture.

photos.caption: Aud's fourth birthday: 9
photos.captionKii: I remember this sweet moment during our cozy family time at home. It looks like we were gathered in the living room, perhaps after opening some gifts, as I can see purple and pink wrapping paper on the couch. My two girls are sharing an affectionate hug, which is just heartwarming to see. The woman on the left is watching the interaction, and my boy is playfully peeking over the back of the couch, observing everyone. It’s lovely to capture these natural, joyful interactions and the relaxed atmosphere of us simply being together.

photos.caption: Pedernales River
photos.captionKii: I remember pausing to capture this tranquil view of a wide, shallow river or creek, flowing between a sandy bank and a distant tree-lined hill. The water has a calm, greenish hue, reflecting the lush green of the trees that dominate the landscape. Above, the sky is a vibrant blue dotted with fluffy white clouds, creating a sense of peace and openness. The dry, grassy patches on the banks and scattered bushes add texture to the scene. It was a beautiful moment of natural serenity I encountered during my journey, a refreshing contrast to some of the urban explorations I'd been enjoying.

photos.caption: Cowboy Up
photos.captionKii: I remember spotting this striking piece of street art on a brick wall, likely during one of my walks. It's a mural depicting the profile of a man wearing a wide-brimmed hat, drawn in a bold, almost graphic novel style with thick outlines. The textured surface of the brick wall really comes through, adding to its character, and I captured it in black and white, which emphasizes its raw, artistic appeal. It's these kinds of unexpected urban discoveries that make exploring new places so interesting.

photos.caption: Little General
photos.captionKii: I remember pausing to capture this interesting metallic fire hydrant on the sidewalk, processed in black and white to really emphasize its texture and form. The way the light catches its silver or gold paint, contrasting with the rough concrete background, made it an eye-catching detail during my walk. It's a common urban fixture, but I found its clean lines and robust design quite photogenic in that moment, especially with the little chain detail adding a touch of industrial charm. It was one of those small, unexpected finds that add character to a city exploration.

photos.caption: Water tower
photos.captionKii: I remember pausing to capture this distinctive water tower, standing tall against the sky. The word "GRUB" is painted boldly on its side, which immediately caught my eye—it felt like such a unique and quirky local detail. The sepia tone and square crop I chose for this photo give it a really timeless, almost vintage postcard feel, which I thought suited the subject perfectly. It's moments like these, discovering the unique character of different places, that truly make a travel journey memorable, and this tower definitely stood out as a local landmark.

photos.caption: Liv's 1st Coke in a bottle
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this playful moment of a young woman taking a big swig from a classic dark glass bottle, possibly a specialty soda, inside what looks like a charming shop or tasting room. Her cheeks are quite rosy, maybe from excitement or the warm atmosphere! In the background, I can see shelves filled with bottles, some stylish lighting fixtures, and other people browsing, which really adds to the lively, local feel of the place. It felt like a fun, candid moment that perfectly summed up the relaxed discoveries I was making during my travels, perhaps a stop at a local vineyard or a uniq

The youngest, with her blonde hair and a pink and purple outfit, is sitting further down the swing. It looks like a bright, warm day, and they're enjoying a casual break outdoors, with a white car parked in the background. This candid shot really adds to the sense of relaxed discov

photos.caption: The Kids in Gruene, TX
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this candid moment of three kids relaxing together on a white wicker loveseat. The boy on the left, with his cheerful cartoon t-shirt, is smiling slightly, while the older girl on the right is casually leaning back, her arm draped behind him, with her foot propped up on a matching wicker stool. The little one in the middle, however, looks like she might be having a grumpy moment, giving a wonderfully expressive little frown! We were on what looks like a lovely outdoor porch or deck, with the wooden floor and some lush greenery in the background. I chose to process this in

photos.caption: My beauties
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this sweet moment between a woman and a young child, who were both looking right at the camera with gentle smiles. The woman, with her sunglasses perched on her head, was leaning her head affectionately against the child, who had beautiful blonde hair and a lovely pink top. The warm lighting and soft focus, almost like a vintage photograph, really highlighted their connection, making it feel like a cherished memory from what might have been a relaxed afternoon during my travels. I can see a glimpse of what looks like an outdoor patio or balcony in the background, adding to

photos.caption: Sun Flower #nofilter
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing the simple beauty of this vibrant yellow sunflower, its petals radiating outwards from the intricate brown center. The bright green leaves frame it perfectly, and I even noticed a small, unopened bud nestled beside it, promising more blooms. It felt like a little burst of sunshine, a lovely natural detail I paused to appreciate and photograph, perhaps in a garden or just along a path during my travels.

photos.caption: bulb
photos.captionKii: I remember being drawn to the simple, raw beauty of this incandescent light bulb, hanging down and casting a warm, soft glow against the dark background. The slightly gritty texture and vignetting around the edges give it a really atmospheric, almost vintage feel. It felt like a small, quiet moment, perhaps a detail I noticed in a room or establishment during my travels, and I wanted to capture its solitary luminescence.

photos.caption: Sea of cabs
photos.captionKii: I remember this quintessential city scene, capturing a line of iconic yellow taxis on what felt like a busy street, with tall, imposing buildings towering in the background. The way the bright sunlight hit the polished surfaces of the cabs and reflected off the windows of the structures really caught my eye, highlighting the vibrant energy of the urban environment. It truly felt like I was right in the middle of the hustle and bustle, a memorable part of exploring this city during my travels.

photos.caption: Globes
photos.captionKii: I remember being charmed by the ambiance of this outdoor space, likely a restaurant patio or a courtyard, with its warm glow of fairy lights and elegant white paper lanterns hanging amongst the trees. The brick building in the background, with its large windows reflecting the indoor lights, added to the cozy evening feel. I can just make out a person leaning on a railing to the left, adding a subtle human presence to this beautifully lit scene. It felt like a perfect spot to relax and enjoy the evening during my travels.

photos.caption: charcuterie @ Norwood — Great smoked/salted meats! Very non-vegetarian. (via Foodspotting)
photos.captionKii: I remember enjoying this fantastic charcuterie board, a perfect meal during my travels. The circular wooden board was generously piled with an assortment of cured meats – delicate slices of ham, spicy salami, and rich marbled cuts. It was beautifully accompanied by toasted baguette slices, tangy cornichons, and what looked like pickled pearl onions, all served with a spoonful of grainy mustard. I even caught a glimpse of my purple sleeve as I was reaching for a piece, making it feel like a very present and enjoyable moment during my journey.

photos.caption: You like apples?
photos.captionKii: I remember spotting this incredibly vibrant, pop-art style apple sculpture, likely in an airport or a large public building, during my travels. The artwork immediately caught my eye with its bold black outlines, bright red and yellow sections, and playful pink polka dots. There's also a distinct green leaf and stem, adding to its whimsical charm. I was drawn to how it added such a splash of color and modern artistry to what seemed like a more functional, subdued background, creating a memorable moment during my journey.

photos.caption: Vegas by night
photos.captionKii: I remember looking out from above during a night flight, captivated by the sprawling tapestry of lights below. It was hard to tell if it was a city or perhaps a large airport with its runways and terminals illuminated, but the intricate patterns against the profound darkness of the night sky were absolutely mesmerizing. The slightly soft focus almost adds to the dreamlike quality of seeing the world from such a height, a quiet moment of reflection during travel.

photos.caption: Les Nubians
photos.captionKii: I remember this energetic night at a concert, where I captured the band mid-performance on stage. The vibrant lighting, especially the rich reds and striking blue-purple spotlight, really set the mood, illuminating the performers in such a dramatic way. There were several talented musicians, including two dynamic female vocalists, one with distinct curly white hair, and a couple of guitarists and a bassist. You can even see the drums partially in the background. The energy in the room was palpable, and I really wanted to remember the excitement of live music.

photos.caption: Cathedral
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this striking shot of a beautiful, gothic-style church, focusing intently on its elegant spires reaching towards what appears to be a dramatic, dark sky. The stark white of the stone architecture, with its intricate details and tall arched windows, really stands out. I was particularly drawn to the pointed tops, each adorned with a small cross, and the way the strong light illuminates the building, creating deep shadows that emphasize its impressive structure. It felt like a moment to appreciate the grandeur and artistry of older architecture.

photos.caption: Flagging interest?
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this striking shot, looking up at the American flag as it unfurled against the bright, possibly overcast sky. What really caught my eye was how the flag, almost translucent in the light, partially revealed the sleek, modern glass building behind it. The building itself is fascinating, with its curved, reflective facade and the distinctive domed, grid-like structure on top, adding a futuristic touch to the urban landscape. It felt like a powerful juxtaposition of patriotism and contemporary architecture.

photos.caption: Old Glory
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing another striking view of the American flag, proudly waving against the backdrop of a towering city building at night. The soft glow from the windows of the skyscraper gives a sense of depth and scale, contrasting with the dark sky. There’s a certain solemnity to seeing the flag illuminated like this, fluttering in the urban night. It was a powerful and memorable moment, truly encapsulating the spirit of the city after dark.

photos.caption: Epic
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this vibrant night scene in a bustling city, truly mesmerized by the towering buildings and the iconic American flag waving proudly in the foreground. The warm glow from the illuminated windows and streetlights contrasts beautifully with the dark night sky, making the city feel alive and energetic. Below the main flag, I can make out other flags, adding to the sense of civic pride and history in this urban landscape. It was a powerful moment, seeing such a display of patriotism set against the backdrop of these impressive structures.

photos.caption: Remember the Alamo
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this close-up detail during my visit to The Alamo in Texas. The rough, weathered texture of the stone block really caught my eye, symbolizing the age and resilience of this historic site. Seeing the words 'THE ALAMO TEXAS' etched into the surrounding stone clearly grounded the moment, reminding me of the profound history contained within these walls. The monochromatic tone of the photo emphasizes the timeless quality of the monument, highlighting its raw, architectural beauty.

photos.caption: Funhouse
photos.captionKii: I remember noticing this fascinating reflection in a building window, capturing a glimpse of an urban landscape. Through the panes, I could clearly see the distinct silhouette of what looks like a large Ferris wheel, its spokes visible, along with other elements of the city skyline reflected in the glass. The dark window frames create a grid, making the reflections appear almost like framed artwork. It was a cool moment, finding such an interesting perspective on the city from inside or across a street.

photos.caption: Squash blossom
photos.captionKii: I remember pausing to admire this delicate light pink flower with its bright yellow center, nestled among green leaves and buds in my garden. The way the sunlight catches its soft petals and casts subtle shadows makes it look truly ethereal. It's moments like these, appreciating the small, beautiful details in my garden, that bring me so much joy.

photos.caption: Vine ripened tomatoes. Yum.
photos.captionKii: I remember the sight of my tomato plants thriving under the warm sun, with a beautiful mix of ripe, reddish-orange tomatoes and vibrant green ones still growing on the vine. It’s always so satisfying to see them at different stages, promising a delicious harvest. The natural light really brings out the textures of the fruit and leaves, and I love how the sturdy metal support helps them grow tall and strong. There’s nothing quite like growing your own food, and these tomatoes were certainly a delight to watch mature in my garden.

photos.caption: Our peas are over the top happy
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this beautiful corner of my garden, showcasing the vibrant sweet pea-like flowers thriving next to the stone wall. On the right, a large bush of bright red blooms is gracefully climbing a circular trellis, adding such a pop of color. To its left, another smaller plant with delicate purple flowers is also climbing, supported by its own trellis. The rich green foliage contrasts beautifully with the colorful petals, and the natural light really highlights their delicate forms. It’s always a joy to see my garden flourishing like this.

photos.caption: Bird of paradise
photos.captionKii: I remember being captivated by the vibrant beauty of this Bird of Paradise flower. Its striking orange and blue petals, nestled within the lush green leaves, always stand out with their unique, almost bird-like shape. The way the natural light catches its distinct form makes it look so alive, truly a spectacular piece of natural art. It was such a lovely moment to just pause and appreciate this tropical bloom, whether it was from my own garden or a nearby botanical spot.

photos.caption: First fruits: two tomatoes & a golden beet from our garden
photos.captionKii: I remember this beautiful spread of fresh produce on my wooden cutting board. There were two vibrant red tomatoes, looking perfectly ripe with their green caps still attached, and a lovely beet or perhaps a small turnip, still sporting its fresh green leafy top and delicate roots. The root vegetable itself had such interesting reddish-yellow stripes! It’s always so satisfying to have such fresh, colorful ingredients ready for cooking, likely straight from the garden or a local market. The natural textures of the wood and the produce really pop in this shot, making me look forward to a healthy

photos.caption: Liv blowing out her candles
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this sweet moment during a birthday celebration. My daughter, the older one, was getting ready to blow out the candles on her Spongebob Squarepants cake – you can see all the candles lit up, ready for her wish! The cake itself was so colorful, with vibrant blues, pinks, and yellows, and all those fun decorations. In the background, her younger sister was sitting patiently (or perhaps a little impatiently, judging by her expression!) on the kitchen counter, watching the scene unfold. It was a classic birthday snapshot, full of anticipation and joy, with a touch of sibling d

photos.caption: The first tomatoes from our garden
photos.captionKii: I remember the satisfying feeling of holding these two perfectly ripe, vibrant red tomatoes right after picking them. They looked so fresh and plump in my hand, with those little green caps still visible. It was a lovely sunny day, and you can see the green grass and some foliage in the background, which really made their color pop. There's nothing quite like harvesting your own produce, and these little gems were such a bright spot.

photos.caption: Party on the bus
photos.captionKii: I remember coming across this really vibrant and striking piece of art or perhaps a decorative light panel. The silhouetted figures of a dancing couple immediately caught my eye, set against a glowing backdrop of warm oranges and yellows. It has such a dynamic feel, almost like they're in mid-twirl. I also noticed the little red stars scattered around and the distinct male and female gender symbols, which added another layer of interest to the piece. The whole thing had a cool, retro-pop aesthetic, and I just loved the bold colors and graphic design against the dark frame.

photos.caption: Morning traffic
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this busy street scene from an elevated perspective, looking down on all the cars navigating the road. What I really loved about this shot was the tilt-shift effect I applied, which makes everything look a bit like a miniature model – it really transforms the scene into something whimsical. There's a prominent white van in the foreground, and you can clearly read the "NO TURNS" painted on the pavement, which is a cool detail. The street was quite active, lined with buildings and trees in the background, all bathed in bright daylight. I found the unique perspective and the

photos.caption: Sunset in SF
photos.captionKii: I remember pulling up to this intersection, the sun was just incredible, either setting or rising directly ahead, creating this dramatic silhouette effect. Everything in front of me – the buildings on both sides, the cars on the road, and even the trees – was cast into deep shadow against the blinding bright sky. The traffic light, glowing red and suspended from its arm, was the only real splash of distinct color against the intense backlight. It was one of those moments where the light completely transforms an ordinary street scene into something almost cinematic, and I just had to capture it

photos.caption: Clocks
photos.captionKii: I remember looking up and capturing this clock tower against what felt like a vast, bright sky. The perspective from below really emphasizes its height and the solid, almost imposing structure of the building. I can see the detailed brickwork or stone facade, rising up to the clock faces on each side, with the hands clearly visible on one of them, indicating it's past 12. There's also some interesting architectural detail on the right, perhaps part of an adjacent building or the tower itself, adding to the urban feel of the shot. It was a clear, bright day, perfect for appreciating the lines a

photos.caption: #socialmediasunset (from a plane)
photos.captionKii: I captured this really breathtaking moment of the sun just breaking through or dipping below the horizon. The sky was an incredible gradient of colors, from a soft, pale yellow at the top, transitioning down to a rich, warm yellow, then a fiery orange, and finally a deep, almost purple-red right at the horizon line where the sun, a bright glowing orb, was visible. The bottom portion of the image is completely silhouetted, which really emphasizes the dramatic sky. It was such a beautiful, serene scene, and I remember feeling completely captivated by the vibrant hues and the sense of vastness.

photos.caption: Holy brush fires, Batman
photos.captionKii: I remember this day, looking out over what looked like a significant plume of smoke rising in the distance. It was a hazy day, with the smoke dominating a good portion of the sky above the houses and trees, making for quite an unsettling sight. We were by the pool, and I can see the lounge chairs and even a young boy, perhaps playing or just observing the unusual scene with me. It felt quite surreal to witness something like that unfolding from a relatively calm poolside setting.

photos.caption: Photo day
photos.captionKii: I remember this bright day seeing this young boy, full of energy and clearly enjoying himself. He's wearing his i9 Sport jersey, holding onto a football, and has such a happy, radiant smile on his face. The background, with all the green and dried grasses, makes it look like we were out on a field or a park, perfect for a game. It was such a great moment to capture him looking so joyful and ready for some outdoor fun.

photos.caption: En Fuego
photos.captionKii: I remember this quiet moment, probably at a restaurant or a cozy spot. I was really drawn to how this single candle glowed inside the clear glass bottle, casting such a warm, inviting light. The deep reds and oranges it created against the dark background were captivating, giving everything a really intimate and calm feel. It was such a simple yet beautiful little detail that caught my eye.

photos.caption: Spider
photos.captionKii: I remember being really captivated by the unique lighting design in this place. All these bare bulbs were hanging down from the ceiling at different lengths, connected by a maze of wires, creating such an interesting, almost industrial-chic look. The black and white filter really brings out the contrast and the architectural lines of the setup. You can even see some intriguing abstract art or texture on the walls in the background, which added to the overall cool atmosphere. It felt very modern and artistic, a detail I definitely wanted to capture.

photos.caption: Ahhh... the ocean!
photos.captionKii: I remember looking out from this spot and really appreciating the view. On the left, there's a light-colored house with a dark roof and prominent windows, framing the scene. In the foreground, a paved path leads to a small brick patio with a wooden picnic table, perfectly situated to enjoy the vast body of water. Across the water, I could faintly make out the distant city skyline under a beautiful blue sky dotted with scattered white clouds, and a clear contrail stretched across the heavens. It was such a peaceful and expansive sight, definitely a moment to capture.

photos.caption: Kings of Leon singing Home Grown
photos.captionKii: I remember this incredible moment from the concert! The stage was absolutely bathed in light, with powerful spotlights cutting through the haze and creating these dramatic beams of light that really emphasized the energy of the performance. You can see the band members scattered across the stage with all their instruments – keyboards, drums, and more – totally immersed in playing. Even from my vantage point, high above the crowd, I could feel the electricity of everyone watching. It was a fantastic live music experience.

photos.caption: Pride
photos.captionKii: I captured this shot of the American flag and the Texas flag flying proudly side-by-side, their fabric gently rippling in the breeze against a soft, overcast sky. It's a classic pairing, representing both national and state pride, and I remember thinking it was a striking visual moment worth capturing.

photos.caption: Epic
photos.captionKii: I was really struck by the dramatic sky this evening and had to capture it. The heavy, dark clouds filled the frame, but there was this incredible burst of bright light in the center, almost as if the sky was glowing from within. Below, I could just make out the blurred lights of the city or a distant town, adding to the moody and atmospheric feel of the whole scene. It was a beautiful, almost ethereal moment.

photos.caption: Rockin the Pre-Commerce
photos.captionKii: This photo really captures the lively atmosphere of our patio hangout! We're all gathered around a long, rustic wooden table, enjoying drinks and deep in conversation. I can see glasses of beer and what looks like iced tea, along with an ashtray and a yellow book titled 'PRE-COMMERCE' resting on the table. In the background, the 'Triple Chimay' sign confirms we're at a pub or restaurant, and there are other people mingling, adding to the bustling energy. It was definitely a great time catching up and relaxing with everyone.

photos.caption: Triangle
photos.captionKii: I spotted this striking triangular window set into an old, weathered brick wall. The unique geometry of the window really stood out against the rustic texture of the bricks. It looks like there's a screen or dark pane over the window, giving it a somewhat mysterious appearance, with just a subtle reflection of what might be an interior ceiling or another building nearby. I love finding these unexpected architectural details.

photos.caption: @Chimoose
photos.captionKii: I snapped this photo of my friend smiling broadly while we were hanging out, likely on a patio somewhere. He's got his sunglasses resting on his head, and in the background, you can make out a wooden fence, an umbrella, and a sign that hints at a pub or restaurant. It looks like we were having a really relaxed and good time.
